segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Forge Road fell away steeply, steps cut straight into living rock by hands that valued efficiency. The air turned wet and metallic; the tunnel smelled of wet granite, old slag, and the copper tang of slow rust. Thornik's lantern burned low, the flame cupped, throwing a disciplined cone of light.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He read the stone as he walked -- hairline fractures, load paths, the cross-rib where an ancient brace took more weight than it should. He laid spring-steel wedges into seams with a quiet click, braces kissing the stone to buy them minutes later when seconds might matter.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The sound from below grew clearer as the wards' dampening effect faded with distance. No longer muffled. Present. Rhythmic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: An hour in, the mountain shivered. Dust fell in silky threads. Everyone stopped on instinct, breath held until the tremor passed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They came to a collapse -- a doorway half-buried by a spill of dwarf-cut granite. Thornik knelt.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Option one: explosive. Brings the ceiling to visit. Option two: levers. Time we don't have."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin stepped beside him, put his right hand on the keystone, and called the smallest heat he could bear -- a focused candle on a pinhead, nothing grand. The scale warmed; the ache sharpened like a pulled muscle. Granite ticked and softened to a bead of glass the crowbar could bite.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik levered. Stone slid. Passage opened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin pulled back, breath hissing. The filaments flared angry red, then settled back to baseline coal-glow. He didn't count what he had left. He didn't know. He only knew there was still enough to hurt and not enough to be careless.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said as she followed him through,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "most men would have tried a bonfire to impress me."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Tragic. I had a speech about stamina prepared."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A narrow twitch at the corner of her mouth -- caught between miffed and entertained.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You're going to be the end of me, Ember-boy."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex called from the far side, one hand pressed to her ribs as if checking they still held.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain answered with a deeper thud that rattled loose grit down Caelin's neck.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A long corridor followed -- clean, quiet, no falls, no scrapes -- only the heartbeat below growing clearer as the wards' bass-trap effect faded with distance. The sound no longer felt distant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It felt present -- not beneath them specifically, but everywhere at once, resonating through the mountain like a heartbeat felt through pressed palms.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In the hush, a shadow-dart whispered along the floor ahead of them, a hair-thin ribbon sliding fast.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It struck an old dwarven inlay they barely saw -- a sliver of ward rune hidden in decorative filigree -- and the floor line erupted in a curtain of sheet-fire two hands high.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dart became a coil of screaming soot-snakes that fled backward into the dark.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Let it keep proving we shouldn't turn around."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Two hours in, they crawled through a chamber where the ceiling had molted down blocks the size of coffins.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Step where I step. Don't even breathe loudly."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's boot knocked a small stone. It skittered. A support block shifted a fraction, and the chamber groaned like a sleeping giant turning over. They sprinted the last ten feet in a pelting rain of grit and pea-stone. Not a full collapse. A reminder.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the pursuit-scrapes paused. Listening. Counting. Herding.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said without looking back. His voice was flat, factual.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we go deeper on our terms,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three hours in, Elowen stopped with her hand to the wall. The rootwork in her arms brightened; a single vine-filament along her wrist blackened another shade, flaking an ash petal onto the floor.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The Weave is screaming ahead. A major brace near a junction is under more stress than it can carry. If it goes while we're under it -- "

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Minutes. Hours. Or it started while I was speaking."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, and winced as her ribs protested the thought.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Four hours in, another choke point. Thornik measured. Levers would work -- eventually. Explosives would kill them. Caelin set his palm to a stone and gave it heat like a match-head. Glass bloomed under his hand; Thornik heaved; the way opened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain threaded Caelin's lattice, not a scream, a serrated sigh. The scale pulsed angry red for three heartbeats, then faded back to coal-glow. Manageable, for now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara fell in beside him again, voice a ribbon of dark amusement.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Refreshing, this restraint. I've been tracking something hot all morning, but I suppose I can exercise patience."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A laugh. Low. Honest. Then the heartbeat underfoot stole the humor back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Five hours in, Serana held a thin, mobile ward over them to shed small falls and carry the sound of their feet away. It flickered once, a brief stutter that she forced flat. She didn't meet anyone's eyes.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's breath rasped under his cheer.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Three-way split: mines, smelters, caldera to surface. The caldera stair is our goal."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Six hours in, the tunnel widened without warning.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They swung into a vast chamber where the walls curved like the inside of a bell.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Bridges of black iron spanned the emptiness -- hundreds of them -- some whole, some snapped mid-arch and hanging by cables older than language. Below, molten seams traced geometric rivers through the dark, glowing like veins under translucent skin.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "This isn't natural construction. It's metallurgy on a planetary scale."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana held her torch close to inscriptions along the nearest bridge rail -- spiral glyphs carved deep and inlaid with gold now blackened by heat. Caelin brushed one with his gloved fingers. It thrummed faintly under the touch, familiar in the way of fever dreams.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex looked down into the molten rivers far below.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Let's try not to puncture anything vital."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum shifted mid-sentence -- Thornik's last word echoed a heartbeat later, repeated perfectly but stretched thin, like parchment under strain.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then Caelin's breath followed, an exhale caught and replayed back to him, slightly off-tempo.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the word came back not once, not twice, but in every voice in the group -- each one slightly wrong. Serana, Vex, Nyxara, Caelin, Thornik, Durgan, Elowen -- all overlapping, all whispering echo in a chorus that didn't belong to any of them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The sound faded like a tide withdrawing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one spoke for a full minute after.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved quickly across one of the intact bridges, giving the central darkness wide berth. The air here tasted of coin and ozone. Light from their torches bled into the rock instead of reflecting, as if the stone drank it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway across, Elowen tilted her head.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "They're trying to remember what they were saying."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air quivered. A dozen faint voices whispered over one another, too faint to catch -- then aligned, forming a single phrase in Draconic that Caelin understood without translation.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Hold the flame. Guard the law. Bury the cost."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The same three lines, repeating in different cadences, layered until they blurred into music.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale in his arm began to glow faintly through his sleeve -- a bright warm pulse of recognition.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "But it doesn't know who to give it to."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik produced a tuning rod from his pack -- a spare, cruder than the one the mountain had shattered two days ago, but it would serve -- ignoring Vex's muttered warning.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "If it's an acoustic record, we can pull more data."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He struck the rod lightly against stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The resulting tone spiraled outward, repeating itself, climbing octaves until the air vibrated visibly. The chamber answered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A column of sound formed in the air -- dense enough to distort the light -- and within it flickered outlines of figures at work: massive shapes, part dragon, part machine, hammering something that shone like molten sunrise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then the image stuttered and bled away, leaving silence so heavy it felt deliberate.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A fine mist of dust fell from the ceiling, glowing faintly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Too late. The sound collapsed inward, a sonic implosion that slammed everyone to their knees. Ears rang. Torches snuffed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When Caelin's vision cleared, Thornik was bleeding from the nose, staring dazed at his shattered rod.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex kicked the broken metal off the platform, grimacing at the pull in her ribs.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara stood very still, eyes half-lidded as if listening inward.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "They're instructions. The mountain replays them when something familiar enters the system. We woke the archive."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Every order the gods ever gave their creations. Every one that failed."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed once -- then again, brighter. The heat licked up Caelin's arm, across his collarbone, behind his eyes. Voices flooded in -- thousands, layered, overlapping. Command structures, prayers, screams of obedience and defiance all at once.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a second, he saw through their memories: dragons kneeling to unseen figures, humans bound by glowing chains, cities forged then unmade by song.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He staggered, gripping the nearest railing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He breathed through it, the images fading like cooling iron.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana caught his other arm, voice sharp.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he lied. The scale flickered white-hot for an instant -- lie detected -- then settled back.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "It recognized him. The system thinks he's one of them."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crossed the chamber quickly, their footsteps echoing in unnatural sync. Each time the echoes came back, they were half a beat too slow -- as if something behind them was learning to walk.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Near the far archway, the air shimmered. At first it was just heat distortion from below. Then it wasn't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He froze. The sound came from behind -- his own tone, precise down to the hesitation between syllables.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Everyone turned. The path behind them was empty.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said automatically, though no one had spoken.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin began, but stopped. His throat had gone dry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik muttered a formula under his breath, trying to anchor the acoustics.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Oh, there's a source. It just borrowed your mouth to speak."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her own name, spoken like a sigh through leaves.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The forest uses names for roots. This isn't the forest."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex snapped, hand moving to a dagger hilt.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then it spoke again -- Serana's voice this time, steady and cold.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik, because curiosity survived even terror, called softly into the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He exhaled, relieved -- then heard it whispered back three times in a child's voice, each repetition younger than the last.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stumbled back hard enough to knock his goggles askew.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air trembled, repeating the words in exact cadence:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Light warped with the next pulse of the hum. The walls sweated condensation that smelled of iron and oil. Their torches flared green, then white. The flame stretched sideways, casting shadows that now mouthed along with the voices.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana tried to summon her wardlight. The sigil formed, flickered, and folded in on itself until only a faint circle of ash remained.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan drew his axe, slow and steady.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The corridor behind them had sealed -- not collapsed, but simply wasn't, replaced by seamless metal still warm to the touch.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Topography editing. It's rewriting as we move."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed once toward the left passage -- hot, insistent. Not the black-violet of corruption, but the bright warm pulse of recognition. Caelin didn't wait for a vote.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Trusting the thing that just tried to eat us?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked, falling in behind him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: As they walked, the walls began to whisper fragments of old conversations: Vex mocking Thornik's kettle, Serana's muttered prayers, Elowen's earlier warning about warmth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain had been listening since Thornwick. Now it was talking back.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The flame mark answers to more than one master,"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I just don't know which is winning."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The corridor opened into another chamber -- a narrow nave lined with glass coffers filled with ash. Light flickered inside each like dying hearts.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then his own voice came from the darkness ahead:

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I called the fire. I killed her to save you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Every muscle in Caelin's body locked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: That sentence didn't exist outside his memory. No one else had ever heard it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana stepped closer, sword half drawn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo stepped forward -- his height, his face, his wound still glowing faintly through translucent skin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He backed away. The others followed his lead, weapons ready but useless against something made of memory.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo tilted its head, movements just slightly off-beat, as if learning how joints worked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When it spoke again, it used all their voices together.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum surged; dust fell in bright spirals. The glass coffers flared, each releasing a puff of ash that drifted like breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin reached for fire, stopped himself, forced the heat inward until it was pain and not power. The scale burned angry red under his skin but he held it, contained it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mimic paused -- as if confused by restraint -- and in that instant Serana hurled her dagger through its chest.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The blade passed through light and hit the wall behind with a sharp, clean clink.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The echo shattered into sound -- hundreds of overlapping syllables that faded into silence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When hearing returned, Vex said softly,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We learn why it knows my name."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They left the chamber in silence, the scale in Caelin's arm fading from red back to baseline coal-glow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the shards of light pulled together, reforming a faint outline.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It whispered after them in Caelin's voice, calm and sure:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They reached the far archway and the space opened like a cathedral of living rock. Three vaulted passages split at precise angles; ribs of stone arced floor to ceiling in calculated load-bearing perfection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik consulted his journal one last time, marked a route with charcoal.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Junction chambers. The caldera route is our way out."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the ribs were cracking. Visible stress fractures spider-webbing across supports that had held for millennia. Dust sifted in steady streams. The air smelled of ozone and pulverized granite.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Deep. Resonant. Felt through boot-soles more than heard.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Seal's heartbeat. Not from one direction. From everywhere. The mountain itself had become its chamber.
